title: ALP board configuration (board.yaml) v1
description: Schema for the per-project board.yaml file that consumers place at their app root.  Declares the target SoM SKU, the carrier (daughter board) it mounts on, per-component assembly overrides, OS backend, inference backend, and optional library / feature enables.  The SDK's loader script (scripts/alp_project.py) reads this file and emits Kconfig fragments (Zephyr), -D flags (plain CMake), or local.conf snippets (Yocto).  See docs/board-config.md.

schema_version.description: Schema revision.  Bumped only on breaking format changes.

som.description: SoM identity -- the *module* the carrier mounts.  Distinct from `carrier` (below): the SoM is fixed at SoM-fab time and its on-module components (silicon, on-module radio, secure element, on-module sensors) cannot be DNI'd after order.  The `overrides` block here covers the rare cases where the SoM ships in variants (e.g. a custom AEN with the OPTIGA Trust M omitted).

sku.description: Stock SoM SKU (MPN).  Resolves to a preset at metadata/e1m_modules/<MPN>/som.yaml.  The SDK ships a preset for every released MPN -- customer's board.yaml just provides the MPN here and everything else (silicon, on-module radio, secure element, RTC, EEPROM, default inference backend) is inherited from the preset.  Recognised: E1M-AEN301/401/501/601/701/801 (Alif Ensemble), E1M-V2N101/V2N102 (Renesas RZ/V2N), E1M-V2M101/V2M102 (V2N + DEEPX), E1M-NX9xxx (NXP i.MX 93, exact SKU TBD).

hw_rev.description: Family-level hardware revision the firmware targets.  Optional; the SDK falls back to the SKU preset's `default_hw_rev` when omitted.  Validated against the family's `hw_revisions` table at metadata/e1m_modules/<family>/hw-revisions.yaml -- the SDK refuses to build when the SDK version (metadata/sdk_version.yaml) sits outside the chosen rev's [min_sdk_version, max_sdk_version] window.  At runtime the SDK reads the rev from the SoM's BOARD_ID ADC channel + resistor divider and aborts boot on mismatch.

overrides.description: Per-on-module-component deltas vs the stock SKU preset.  Only covers components actually populated on the SoM PCB -- carrier-side sensors (IMU, barometer, OLED, camera) go in `carrier.populated` instead.

wifi_ble.description: On-module Wi-Fi 6 + BLE 5.4 radio.  AEN: TI CC3501E.  V2N family: Murata LBEE5HY2FY (Infineon CYW55513).

secure_element.description: On-module hardware secure element backing <alp/security.h>'s ECC + AES + TRNG path.  Only the AEN family ships one by default.

rtc_external.description: On-module external RTC.  AEN has RV-3028-C7; V2N relies on the SoC-internal RTC.

ethernet.description: On-module Ethernet PHY (RTL8211FDI-VD-CG on V2N family; AEN has the MAC routed but no on-module PHY -- the carrier provides one when present).

memory.description: Memory subsystem capacities -- per-SKU presets fill these from the released parts list; overrides cover custom-DRAM-population variants of the same SoM SKU.

dram_mbit.description: LPDDR4X / LPDDR5X capacity in megabits (e.g. 32 Gbit = 32768).

flash_mbit.description: Boot flash capacity in megabits.  eMMC on V2N family, SPI-NAND on AEN.

mram_kbit.description: On-die MRAM capacity (Alif Ensemble only; ignored on V2N/N93).

carrier.description: Carrier board the SoM is soldered onto.  Distinct from the SoM (above): the carrier defines which sensors, OLEDs, cameras, mic/speaker amps, current monitors, and other parts are populated -- and on custom carriers, which ones are intentionally DNI'd.  Omit this block entirely if the application doesn't reach beyond the SoM (e.g. a headless inference-only firmware running on a custom carrier that just exposes power + serial).

name.description: Carrier board name.  Stock values resolve to a preset under metadata/carriers/<name>.yaml: 'E1M-EVK' (35x35 EVK for AEN / N93 SoMs), 'E1M-X-EVK' (45x65 EVK for V2N / V2N-M1 SoMs).  Custom carriers use any unique name + skip the preset; declare all populated components in `populated`.

hw_rev.description: Carrier hardware revision.  Optional; falls back to the carrier preset's `default_hw_rev` when omitted.  Validated against the carrier preset's `hw_revisions` table at metadata/carriers/<name>/board.yaml in the same way the SoM `hw_rev` is.  Read at runtime from a carrier-side BOARD_ID ADC + resistor divider.

populated.description: Per-component populated flags.  Keys match the chip-driver names (lsm6dso, bmi323, icm42670, bmp581, bme280, lis2dw12, ssd1306, ssd1331, ov5640, pdm_mic, tas2563, ina236, cam_mux_pi3wvr626, tcal9538, ...).  When `carrier.name` references a preset, this block applies as a delta -- only list keys you're overriding (e.g. `lsm6dso: false` to DNI on a custom variant).  Each `true` enables the corresponding CONFIG_ALP_SDK_CHIP_<name> in the resulting build.

os.description: Target OS backend.  Maps to ALP_OS in plain-CMake, to the prj.conf in Zephyr, to the MACHINE in Yocto.

inference.description: Inference backend selection.  Defaults pick the best available NPU on the SoM; explicit values override.

default_arena_kib.description: TFLM tensor arena size in KiB.  Apps override per-call via alp_inference_config_t.arena_bytes.

libraries.description: Optional libraries to make available to app code.  When listed, the SDK adds the library's include path + the matching compile-time profile (under metadata/library-profiles/<lib>/) so the library is set up correctly for the SDK's embedded invariants -- no exceptions, no <iostream>, no STL on M-class, no broken ciphers in MbedTLS, etc.  Apps use the library through its native API (e.g. `#include "etl/vector.h"`, `lv_label_create(...)`, `nlohmann::json::parse(...)`), NOT through any <alp/...> wrapper.

iot.description: Connectivity feature toggles.  Enabling iot.wifi pulls in the Wi-Fi station path; iot.mqtt the MQTT client; iot.ble the Bluetooth host stack; iot.tls the MbedTLS-backed <alp/security.h> path.

last_error.description: Thread-local alp_last_error() slot.  Disable to save a few bytes of TLS on extremely-tight builds.

oneOf.description: Specific part number for a custom-populated variant.
